agents.cap.equivhint: the cap's equivalent on other hosts (Tramalia doesn't configure them):
agents.cap.set: model cap set: {cap} — applied to the subagents
agents.none: no subagents (.claude/agents/) — did you run `tramalia init`?
close.done.nogates: task {task} closed with a documented EXCEPTION (no gates — install mise for real validation).
close.uninit: this project is not initialized: there is no convention to govern. Run `tramalia init` first.
context.set.needname: missing name: tramalia context set <backend> (see `context list`)
context.set.ok: context backend set: {name} — agents read it from tools.json
context.util.header: point-in-time utilities (don't compete with the backend):
doctor.fix.pick: select what to install (space marks, enter confirms)
doctor.path.uv.missing: uv tools (~/.local/bin) aren't on your PATH — run `uv tool update-shell` (or in the TUI: i → configure PATH), then restart the terminal
doctor.pathfix.done: uv PATH configured — restart the terminal for it to take effect
doctor.viago: installed via go (~/go/bin) — add it to PATH if your shell lacks it
doctor.viamise: installed via mise (shims) — activate with `mise activate` or restart the terminal
doctor.viauv: installed via uv (~/.local/bin) — add it to PATH with `uv tool update-shell`
menu.title: what do you want to do?
offer.later: you can install it later with: pip install {paquete}
offer.manual: manual: pip install {paquete}  ·  with pipx: pipx inject tramalia-cli {paquete}
skills.add.duplicada: that skill is already in the catalog (use enable if it's disabled)
skills.add.ok: skill added to the manifest: {name} — run sync (s) to clone it
skills.add.url-invalida: invalid URL: must be http(s):// or git+…
skills.install.nogit: ✗ git is missing to clone external skills
skills.outdated.checking: checking for updates on the remotes (git ls-remote)…
skills.outdated.hint: installed version after @; `tramalia skills outdated` compares with the remote.
skills.outdated.none_installed: no installed external skills to check.
skills.outdated.update_all: update with `tramalia skills sync` (all) or `tramalia skills sync <name>` (one).
skills.outdated.uptodate: all installed external skills are up to date.
skills.rehydrate: declared but not cloned: {names} — run `tramalia skills` to fetch them (they're not in the repo).
skills.sync.notdeclared: '{name}' isn't declared in skills.toml (enable it with `tramalia skills enable {name}`).
skills.toggle.fail: couldn't recognize the '{name}' block in skills.toml — edit it by hand (nothing was touched)
skills.tracked.fix: remove them from the index without deleting: git rm -r --cached .tramalia/skills/<name> (the .gitignore already prevents re-adding).
skills.tracked.warn: ⚠ external skills committed to git (shouldn't be uploaded, they're heavy): {names}
skills.toggle.on: enabled in skills.toml: {name} — run sync to clone it
skills.update.checking: checking for updates on the remotes…
skills.update.found: {n} external skill(s) with an update available (Enter on one to update it).
tui.audit.empty: no closes recorded yet — close your first task with `tramalia close`.
tui.audit.uninit:
this project is not initialized: there is no audit to show.
Go to Overview and press the Initialize button (or `tramalia init`).
tui.close.agent.placeholder: who EXECUTED the task — audit record only (Tramalia doesn't invoke it)
tui.close.done.nogates: [yellow]○ task closed with a documented EXCEPTION (no gates — install mise for real validation)[/yellow]
tui.close.model.placeholder: optional: model name, e.g. claude-opus-4-8 (recorded in `tramalia log`, doesn't block the close)
tui.close.nogates: [yellow]▲ gates NOT run (mise missing) — will be recorded as a documented exception[/yellow]
tui.close.reviewer.placeholder: who REVIEWS the close — audit record only
tui.close.taskinfo.none: that task is not in specs/tasks.md — add it there so the close is traceable.
tui.close.uninit:
this project is not initialized — there is no convention to govern.
Initialize it first:
tui.ctxbackend.line: context backend: {name} (active) — change with the b key
tui.ctxbackend.notinstalled: ○ not installed — install it (i key) or pick another
tui.ctxbackend.oknotinstalled: backend {name} set, but it's NOT installed — install it (i key) or pick another
tui.ctxbackend.title: choose the project's active context backend
tui.docs.none: this row has no associated documentation
tui.install.admin: ▲ {tool} seems to need an ADMINISTRATOR terminal — open it elevated and retry
tui.install.cancelled: ✋ {tool} cancelled — moving on to the next one
tui.install.done: [green]✓ mise install finished (check the refreshed table)[/green]
tui.install.chained: ↳ {tool} is now unblocked — installing it next
tui.install.manual.header: require manual install (not automatable on your system):
tui.install.nomise:
[yellow]mise is not installed (it's bootstrap: it can't install itself).[/yellow]
Install it for your system and try again:
  Windows:     winget install jdx.mise
  Linux/macOS: curl https://mise.run | sh
  macOS:       brew install mise
tui.install.none: nothing can be installed automatically; check the manual alternatives in the table.
tui.install.timeout: ⏱ {tool} hit the time limit — process terminated, moving on
tui.install.title: install missing — space to mark, enter to confirm
tui.skills.col.name: skill
tui.skills.hint: States: ✓ installed (◍ declared = in the manifest, not cloned · ○ available).  Enter installs (or updates if already there) · s updates all · u checks updates · d opens docs · paste a git URL above to add.
tui.skills.sync.none: no external skills declared (enable one with Enter).
tui.skills.uninit: uninitialized project: no skills to manage (use ⚙ Initialize in Close).
tui.skills.url.placeholder: paste a skill's git URL and press Enter to add it
tui.state.uninitialized: [yellow]NOT initialized — use the Initialize button or `tramalia init`[/yellow]
tui.subtitle: repo-first governance and evidence
